summary:
Rapid prototyping encompasses various methods such as laser curing using ultraviolet lasers, Selective Laser Sintering which involves heating particles until molten then cooling rapidly back down again; also includes Fused Deposition Modelling whereby melted material gets laid out layer by layer through an extruder nozzle during construction processes leading up towards finished products or models quickly without needing traditional toolings like molds etc...
The selected answer aligns correctly reflecting comprehensive coverage over multiple types utilized within this field thus validating its accuracy based upon reviewed sources presented here today... Agent1 concludes their summary focusing primarily on different methodologies used under umbrella term 'Rapid Prototyping', emphasizing specific examples from retrieved documentation while ensuring alignment between options given & factual content derived directly related questions posed initially regarding truthfulness amongst choices offered earlier...

To summarize:

- **Laser Curing**: Uses UV light to cure photopolymer resin.
- **Selectively Laser Sintering** (SLS): Heats powder materials locally so parts fuse together but remain loose enough not yet fully solidified.
- **Fused Deposition Modeling** (FDM): Extrudes thermoplastic filament continuously building object one thin slice at time stacked vertically upwards forming final product shape efficiently utilizing - **Fused Deposition Modeling** (FDM): Extrudes thermoplastic filament continuously building object one thin slice at time stacked vertically upwards forming final product shape efficiently utilizing
